topic: Two-pole geomagnetic model — change-driven re-review (c4); most c3 recommendations applied, residual issues
change_summary: Since c3 (2026-04-18): detail_evidence grew 3872→4958 chars (+28%). Changes incorporate c3 hole #1 (pure-dipole ~60,700 nT correction), hole #2 (Griffiths §5.3.3 demoted with nuance paragraph), hole #3 (dome-side strawman softened to "frames as"), and hole #6 (§3.5.8 cross-ref added). Verdict unchanged (Self-Contradicted). detail_verdict_length unchanged (2061).
our_argument_summary: WIN-053: Dome claims Closed Toroidal Ovoid geometry produces dipole-like field via sub-terrestrial return path. Verdict Self-Contradicted. Core argument unchanged from c3: flux conservation within dome's own geometry requires B_south ≈ 39 nT at 500 km aperture (156 nT at 1000 km) versus fitted 64,852 nT — a 1,660×/415× discrepancy. Supporting: coefficients fit WMM2025 pole strengths without derivation; 20% RMS is ~200× worse than IGRF-13; shared λ=8,619 km across H(r) and B(r) with no independent derivation; V50.6→V51.0 refactor introduced a self-contradiction absent from the simpler monopolar predecessor; PRED-TOROID-002 registered 2026-04-04 but confirmation dated 2026-03-23 (retrodiction).

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt (V50.6 list has only 39 WINs; no WIN-053)
raw-text/05-model.txt (V50.6 — monopolar vortex only, no toroidal model)
raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 22 (confirms λg=8,619 km aetheric scale length)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No monitoring for WIN-053. monitor.py retains 39 V50.6 domains; no domain was added for WIN-053 when V51.0 introduced it. The B(r) equation, 62,376/64,852 coefficients, and 20%-RMS claim are static prose on the V51 website — not computed from any live fetch.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: No monitoring exists. Static HTML prose only.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Two fitted coefficients (62,376 and 64,852 nT) and a shared scale λ = 8,619 km can be retuned to any observed dipole. The aetheric refractive index n(r) remains in reserve for any positional/angular discrepancies. Sub-terrestrial return 'narrow channel' reinterpretation is the main unpreempted escape (see Hole H2).
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None in the dome_code_analysis section. But note: detail_verdict_text invokes 'hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call' — this is factually wrong for WIN-053 because no monitoring exists. See Hole H1.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Verified via grep of raw-text/02-wins.txt (V50.6 dump contains 39 WINs, no WIN-053) and raw-text/05-model.txt (no toroidal architecture in V50.6). monitor.py was not extended when V51.0 added WIN-053. No INTERMAGNET/CHAOS-7/Swarm fetch validates B(r).
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Earth dipole field is described by IGRF-13 (degree 13 spherical harmonic expansion) and CHAOS-7 with ~50 nT RMS. Dome relabels the geodynamo as aetheric toroidal circulation but predicts nothing these models don't already predict with ~200× better accuracy.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: B(r) coefficients ≈ observed pole strengths (62,376 ≈ 58–60 kT claimed north per text / real IGRF-13 ~54–56 kT; 64,852 ≈ claimed 65–67 kT south / IGRF ~66 kT). Pole strengths have been measured continuously since Gauss (1838).
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Only λ = 8,619 km connects to a dome parameter (shared with H(r)). The two coefficients 62,376 and 64,852 have no geometric derivation — they are fits. Moreover λ itself is fit from H(r), which is fit from Polaris elevation observations through the aetheric medium whose refractive index bends light (Section 1.8 coordinate-collapse argument) — λ is fitted-to-contaminated-data.

holes_found.description: CATEGORY-ERROR BOILERPLATE IN detail_verdict_text. The second paragraph of detail_verdict_text says: 'Under the dome author's own definition ('prediction was timestamped before confirming data was pulled'), the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call, making even that weaker test unverifiable.' This is a categorical statement about monitor.py behavior that contradicts our own dome_code_analysis finding for this WIN: 'monitor.py does not track WIN-053, and no script computes B(r).' There is no hardcoded expected value for WIN-053 and no live fetch call for it. The paragraph reads like boilerplate copy-pasted from another WIN's verdict text without adaptation. A dome defender will spot this instantly: either we know monitor.py tracks WIN-053 (in which case the rest of our analysis is wrong) or we don't (in which case this sentence is invented). This is the sort of internal inconsistency we criticize the dome for.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite the second paragraph to reflect that WIN-053 is not monitored at all. Suggested: 'Independent of the scientific critique, this claim also fails our post-hoc test on structural grounds. The numerical values match observed magnetic pole field strengths published for decades (trivial-transcription test, Part 3.1b). No legitimate-novelty carve-out applies: the fitted coefficients are not obscure, and the dome provides no derivation that would constitute genuine prediction. Note that WIN-053 has no monitor.py domain at all — it appears on the predictions site as static prose, so the usual timestamping test does not apply. See Part 3.1b.' This preserves the Part 3.1b alignment without the factually wrong monitor.py claim.

holes_found.description: ADVOCATE-MODE 'SUB-TERRESTRIAL RETURN CHANNEL' DEFENSE STILL UNPREEMPTED. c3 identified this at rating 3/5 and recommended a preemption paragraph. The c3→current rewrite added the Griffiths nuance paragraph (c3 hole #2) but did NOT close the narrow-return-channel escape hatch. A charitable dome defender will argue: 'The 1,600:1 area ratio assumes the return spans the whole disc. In fact the return is a narrow sub-crustal channel whose area is comparable to the polar aperture — flux is preserved in that narrow tube; the B(r) equation describes what surface observers see, not the sub-terranean flux density.' This is not refutable without an explicit counter: (a) the dome validates its 20% RMS against surface-observatory data (INTERMAGNET/CHAOS-7), so 64,852 nT is being proposed as a SURFACE value; (b) if the return were truly channelized to a narrow tube, the dome would owe us the channel cross-section and a mechanism by which a narrow sub-crustal flux of ~65 kT produces the ~55 kT measured at the surface through thousands of km of rock — which is standard globe-physics territory.
holes_found.recommendation: Add one paragraph to detail_evidence closing this defense, per c3 advocate_mode preemptive_recommendation (verbatim text already drafted in the c3 review JSON). This is the single most important addition the text still needs.

holes_found.description: '168 spherical harmonic coefficients' FOR IGRF-13 IS NUMERICALLY OFF. IGRF-13 main field extends to degree n=13, giving Σ(2n+1) for n=1..13 = 195 Gauss coefficients. 168 corresponds to degree n=12 truncation (Σ(2n+1) for n=1..12). This is probably an honest confusion between 'IGRF-13' as the generation label vs the degree. Minor imprecision; doesn't affect the argument (195 vs 168 is still vastly better than dome's 3-parameter fit).
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite as: 'from 195 Gauss coefficients (degree 13 spherical harmonic expansion) grounded in geodynamo physics...' Or, if you want to avoid recomputing coefficient count, soften to 'from a high-order spherical harmonic expansion (degree 13)...'

holes_found.description: Sub-H(r) = H(r)×(1 − e^(−r/δ)) WITH δ = 6,371 km IS UNSOURCED. This formula appears in detail_extra with the observation 'notably, this is Earth's mean radius, a globe-derived constant with no derivation from dome geometry.' But our V50.6 raw-text archive does not contain WIN-053 content, and no URL/anchor is given to the V51 dome page that supposedly states this formula. If the dome does not literally use δ=6,371 km, we are strawmanning a specific formula. The globe-fingerprint observation is rhetorically valuable but only if the formula is verbatim dome text.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) add a direct quote plus URL/anchor (e.g., https://john09289.github.io/predictions/model.html#WIN-053) and archive.org snapshot, or (b) soften to 'the dome's Sub-H(r) function uses a scale factor matching Earth's mean radius (~6,371 km) — a globe-derived constant.' The archival capture recommended in c3 stronger_arguments should cover this as well.

holes_found.description: NORTH POLE FIELD STRENGTH OVERSTATED. Current text says coefficient A=62,376 nT 'closely matches observed magnetic pole field strengths: ~58,000–60,000 nT at the north magnetic pole.' The North Magnetic Pole total field intensity per recent IGRF-13 / WMM2025 is ~54,000–56,000 nT, not 58–60 kT. The 58-60 kT range overstates the match between the fitted coefficient and the observed value. The south coefficient (64,852 nT vs ~66 kT at the South Magnetic Pole) is approximately correct. This doesn't change the argument — even with the corrected ~55 kT figure, 62,376 is still closely 'in the neighborhood' of a known observed value — but the specific numeric claim is wrong and a dome defender may pick at it.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite as: 'A = 62,376 nT and C = 64,852 nT sit in the range of observed magnetic pole field strengths: ~54,000 nT at the North Magnetic Pole and ~66,000 nT at the South Magnetic Pole per WMM2025/IGRF-13.'

holes_found.description: c3 HOLE #4 (3,000 km APERTURE SENSITIVITY) STILL UNAPPLIED. Adding one more sensitivity row would close the 'but what if the aperture is huge' quibble permanently. Verified: at r_pole=3,000 km, area ratio ≈ 44.5:1 → B_south bound ≈ 1,401 nT → discrepancy ≈ 46× — still catastrophic even at a pole-radius 6× beyond anything the dome plausibly claims.
holes_found.recommendation: Add to the sensitivity sentence: 'Even at a 3,000 km aperture — six times the original estimate and larger than any geometric feature the dome describes for the Axis Mundi — the ratio is still ~45:1, bounding B_south ≤ ~1,400 nT, ~46× below the fitted value.'

holes_found.description: c3 HOLE #5 (FLUX DIRECTION SOURCE VS SINK) STILL UNAPPLIED. Flux conservation constrains both magnitude AND direction. The dome's fitted B(r) gives two similar-magnitude maxima but does not enforce that the two ends are oppositely-directed with respect to the return surface. In a true closed circuit, the two surface maxima must have opposite flux sense; the dome's equation is silent on this.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional one-line addition to detail_evidence: 'Flux conservation additionally requires the two surface peaks to be oppositely directed with respect to the sub-terranean return; the dome's B(r) fit enforces no such directional constraint, compounding the self-contradiction.' c3 flagged this as minor; it remains minor, but applying it costs one sentence.

stronger_arguments.description: λ IS DOUBLY FITTED AND FITTED TO CONTAMINATED DATA. λ = 8,619 km is fit from H(r), which is fit from Polaris elevation observations passing through the aetheric medium whose refractive index is claimed to bend sunlight by ~1,500 km apparent altitude (Section 1.8). If n(r) is strong enough to displace the sun's apparent position, it displaces Polaris too — so the angles used to fit H(r) (and thus λ) are contaminated. The 'zero fitted parameters' framing some dome pages use for WIN-053 is doubly false: (a) the two coefficients are fits, and (b) the 'shared' λ is itself a fit to contaminated data.
sources: raw-text/05-model.txt lines on Polaris-elevation H(r) derivation

stronger_arguments.description: ARCHIVAL CAPTURE OF PRED-TOROID-002 TIMELINE. The retrodiction claim (confirmed 2026-03-23, registered 2026-04-04) is timestamp-based and durability-sensitive. c3 recommended archive.org / archive.today snapshots of the dome predictions page at both dates. Not applied yet. A single footnote with a Wayback Machine URL preempts any silent backdating of PRED-TOROID-002.
sources: Poller change logs in monitor/changes/ that captured first observation of PRED-TOROID-002

stronger_arguments.description: SURFACE-VS-SUBSURFACE EXPLICIT. The dome's 20% RMS improvement is computed against surface-observatory data. Making this explicit in detail_evidence closes both the Advocate-Mode narrow-channel defense and the 'maybe 64,852 is sub-terranean' escape in one sentence. The evidence paragraph is already long; one clause — 'the dome's own 20% RMS is computed against surface-observatory measurements (INTERMAGNET/CHAOS-7 validation stations), so the 64,852 nT south-pole term is a surface value by the dome's own validation method' — does the work.

kernel_of_truth.description: Unchanged from c3. The toroidal architecture is a real improvement over V50.6's monopolar vortex: a ring-magnet / closed-circuit topology is the correct direction for a dipole-like field, and recognizing ∇·B = 0 at all is more than many heterodox cosmologies do. The shared scale length λ is a parsimony move an honest physicist would at least attempt, and the 61% → 20% RMS quantification engages with empirical validation.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The execution fails by the dome's own rules. Flux conservation — the very principle motivating a closed circuit — forbids the fitted equation because the north-aperture and return areas differ by ~1,600:1. The exponential form matches no standard closed-circuit geometry. The coefficients 62,376 and 64,852 are observed pole strengths relabeled. The 20% RMS is ~200× worse than IGRF-13 with a century of geodynamo physics. The architectural improvement was adopted qualitatively, then fitted to the measurements it claims to predict.

advocate_mode.best_defense: 'Your 1,600:1 area ratio assumes the return flux is spread across the full sub-disc area. In fact the "Sump" is a constrained sub-crustal channel whose cross-section is comparable to the polar aperture — flux is preserved in that narrow tube. The B(r) equation is an observer formula describing what surface observers see, not a claim about the sub-terranean flux density. You cannot refute our architecture by assuming a geometry we didn't claim.' (Variation: 'We never said the return is uniform across the whole disc. The exponential decay itself describes the field falling off outside the narrow return channel.')
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Close this defense by making two observations explicit in detail_evidence: (i) the dome's own 20% RMS improvement is computed against surface-observatory measurements (INTERMAGNET/CHAOS-7 stations), so the 64,852 nT south-pole term is being proposed as a SURFACE value by the dome's own validation method — a sub-terranean reinterpretation disconnects the equation from the data the dome uses to validate it; (ii) if the return flux were truly channelized to a narrow sub-crustal tube, the dome owes us the channel cross-section and a mechanism by which a narrow sub-crustal flux of ~65 kT produces ~55 kT measured at the surface through thousands of kilometers of rock — which is standard globe-physics territory, not dome physics. See Hole H2. (Other side: a dome defender COULD legitimately argue that we're uncharitable in assuming full-disc return without asking; that's why the SURFACE-validation point is load-bearing — it rebuts the charitable reading without us having to assume a specific return geometry.)

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: No cross-WIN contradictions. WIN-053's criticism of the fitted coefficients reinforces WIN-012 (κ=1.67 nT/μGal fit using the same λ). WIN-013/014 eclipse-null arguments are consistent with treating κ as operationally empty. WIN-037's field-decay argument is consistent with pole strengths being observed rather than predicted. WIN-058b's Bedrock Induction Ratio κ=1.677 uses the same λ=8,619 km machinery; if λ is unjustified here it is unjustified there. One consistency nit: WIN-053's Hole H1 (monitor.py category error in detail_verdict_text) is a pattern to audit across other WINs — if this boilerplate was copy-pasted, WIN-012 and WIN-058b may have the same bug. Recommend a grep across wins.json for 'hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit' before patching.

claims_checked: A_pole(3000) = π×3000² ≈ 2.83×10⁷ km² — area ratio ≈ 44.5 (for proposed new sensitivity row)
B_south(3000) ≈ 1,401 nT, discrepancy ≈ 46× (for new sensitivity row)
IGRF-13 coefficient count: Σ(2n+1) for n=1..13 = 195 (NOT 168; 168 = degree 12)
quantitative_verification.computations: Python (math module) — all flux-conservation arithmetic and dipole computations verified. New sensitivity point at 3,000 km aperture verified. Coefficient count claim 168 identified as off-by-one-degree: 168 corresponds to n=1..12 truncation, 195 to full IGRF-13 n=1..13 main field.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: (1) '168 spherical harmonic coefficients' is the degree-12 count; IGRF-13 main field uses degree 13 = 195 coefficients. Minor. (2) '~58,000–60,000 nT at the north magnetic pole' overstates the IGRF-13 value by ~3–6 kT; actual is ~54–56 kT. Minor. (3) All flux-conservation arithmetic verified correct. (4) Standard dipole at θ=170° verified at 60,710 nT, matching review text's 60,700 nT.

deeper_analysis:
Cycle 4 assessment: the c3→c4 interval saw the three moderate c3 holes successfully addressed — pure-dipole number corrected (~60,700 nT), Griffiths overreach demoted to a subordinate nuance paragraph, dome-side 'zero field' strawman softened to 'frames as.' The §3.5.8 cross-reference was also added. Good progress.

One major hole remains active: H1, the detail_verdict_text boilerplate claim about monitor.py hardcoded-with-live-fetch. For WIN-053 there is no monitoring at all, so this sentence is factually wrong on its own terms and contradicts our own dome_code_analysis. This is the single most important fix for c4 because it is the kind of self-inconsistency we criticize the dome for; a skim-level defender will weaponize it. The fix is a paragraph-level rewrite (drafted in the recommendation).

One moderate hole remains active: H2, the sub-terrestrial-return-channel advocate defense still unpreempted. This was flagged at 3/5 defense_survives in c3 with a specific preemption recommendation that was not applied. The fix is adding one paragraph — not a rewrite.

Four minor holes (H3–H7): IGRF-13 coefficient count (168→195 or softer language), Sub-H δ sourcing, north-magnetic-pole field strength overstated (58-60k→54-56k), c3 aperture-sensitivity 3000 km row not added, c3 flux-direction sentence not added. All are paragraph- or sentence-level edits.

Stronger-argument opportunities (λ refraction contamination, archive.org PRED-TOROID-002 snapshot, surface-vs-subsurface explicit) are still open.

Quantitative verification: all flux-conservation arithmetic checks out. Two numerical claims off (IGRF-13 coefficient count; north-pole field strength); both minor and neither undermines the argument.

Cross-WIN: no inconsistencies with WIN-012, WIN-013, WIN-014, WIN-037, WIN-058b. One audit recommendation: grep for the H1 boilerplate pattern across other WINs — if copy-pasted, the same category-error may contaminate other detail_verdict_text sections.

Recommended_action: minor_edit. H1 is a single-paragraph rewrite, H2 is a paragraph addition, H3–H7 are sentence-level. The argument structure is sound; the text needs tightening.

Other-side note (per steve's preference): A fair dome defender who accepts the flux-conservation argument in isolation might still push back on three fronts I find partially sympathetic: (a) comparing a 3-parameter dome fit to a 168-or-195-parameter IGRF is statistically unfair — IGRF is orders of magnitude richer by construction; (b) the dome's core claim (dipole-like surface field) is trivially achievable and the 20% RMS number might be a meaningful step up from V50.6's 61% regardless of the architecture critique; (c) our criticism that λ is 'shared across H(r) and B(r)' cuts both ways — a physicist might praise this as parsimony rather than attack it as double-use. None of these defenses save the Self-Contradicted verdict (flux conservation still forbids the fit on dome-geometry terms), but they're worth keeping in mind when writing the text — our tone should be 'the dome tried something genuinely interesting and it broke on its own math' rather than 'everything the dome does is stupid.' Current detail_verdict_text's opening sentence ('genuine improvement over V50.6') already does this — preserve it.

citations_verified: South Atlantic Anomaly EPSL DOI: https://doi.org/10.1016/j.epsl.2018.12.019 — resolves to Finlay et al. SAA paper
Griffiths, Introduction to Electrodynamics, §5.3.3 Example 5.12 — correctly characterized as applying to tightly-wound toroidal coils; dome-geometry caveat now in text
citations_failed: Sub-H(r) formula with δ=6,371 km (detail_extra) — no URL or dome-site anchor provided. V50.6 raw-text does not contain this formula; V51 source unverified. See H4.
citations_unchecked: Dome's claimed 61% → 20% RMS improvement — originates on the dome site, not independently recomputed. Text presents this as dome-internal claim.
'168 spherical harmonic coefficients' for IGRF-13 — the actual coefficient count for IGRF-13 main field (degree 13) is 195; 168 is the degree-12 count. See H3.
'~58,000–60,000 nT at the north magnetic pole' — IGRF-13/WMM2025 value is closer to ~54,000–56,000 nT. See H5.
Antikythera analog (referenced in WIN-020 not WIN-053; noted here only as N/A)
